title: Increase in permanent residence fees effective April 30, 2022 - Canada.ca
content:
## Increase in permanent residence fees effective April 30, 2022
Ottawa, April 5, 2022 – On April 30, 2022, Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) is increasing fees for all permanent residence applications. This includes economic, permit holder, family and humanitarian classes.
In 2020, IRCC increased permanent residence fees to account for inflation for the first time since 2002. At that time, it was announced that fees would increase every 2 years to adjust for inflation.
The Government of Canada supports a cost-effective approach to financing government programs, where most of the costs are the responsibility of those who receive the services and benefit directly from them.
New Fee April 30, 2022
Right of Permanent Residence Fee
Principal applicant and accompanying spouse or common-law partner
Federal High Skilled, Provincial Nominee Program and Quebec Skilled Workers, Atlantic Immigration Class and most Economic Pilots (Rural, Agri-Food)
Accompanying spouse or common-law partner
Accompanying dependent child
Live-in Caregiver Program and Caregivers Pilots (Home Child Provider Pilot and Home Support Worker Pilot)
Accompanying spouse or common-law partner
Accompanying dependent child
Business (Federal and Quebec)
Accompanying spouse or common-law partner
Accompanying dependent child
Family Reunification (Spouses, Partners and Children; Parents and Grandparents; and other relatives)
Sponsored principal applicant
Sponsored dependent child
Accompanying spouse or common-law partner
Accompanying dependent child
Accompanying spouse or common-law partner
Accompanying dependent child
Humanitarian and Compassionate / Public Policy
Accompanying spouse or common-law partner
Accompanying dependent child
